Noun paradigms for the Ringworld-era Kxaki language. See [[Ringworld culture building]] for the sound system and general grammar, and [[Ringworld basic syntax words]] for the basic vocabulary these examples are built on.

== Overview ==

There are '''eight declensions''', formed by crossing two stem shapes (vowel-ended, consonant-ended) with four genders:

* '''inanimate''' (uncountable / mass)
* '''child'''
* '''feminine'''
* '''masculine'''

The language uses '''tripartite alignment''': the subject of a transitive verb, the object of a transitive verb, and the sole argument of an intransitive verb are each marked distinctly.

[[File:Cat genders in Old Aelaki.svg|thumb|Gender endings sketched on the root ''cat'']]

== Cases ==

Cases are layered: if a declension uses one of these, it generally uses all of the ones listed above it.

# '''Subject''' (transitive agent) — ''-ɓi''
# '''Object''' (transitive patient) — ''-la/-na''
# '''Intransitive''' — unmarked
# '''Inalienable possessive''' (genitive) — ''-bu''
# '''Alienable possessive''' — ''-dzh''
# '''Instrumental / ablative''' — ''-z''
# '''Vocative''' — ''-go''
# '''Dative''', '''locative''', '''comitative''' — used in fuller paradigms

== Number ==

Three numbers, matching the Ringworld plural system:

* '''singular''' — unmarked
* '''non-exhaustive plural''' — full reduplication of the stem; "some of the X"
* '''exhaustive plural''' — suffix ''-l'' (with connecting vowel matching gender); "all the X"

== Consonant-ended paradigms ==

Exemplified with the stem '''mak''' ("language").

=== Child ===

{| class="wikitable"
|+ ''mak'' — child declension
! Number !! Subject !! Object !! Intrans. !! Inal. poss. !! Alien. poss. !! Instr. !! Voc.
|-
! singular
| makɓi || makla || mak || makbu || makydzh || makyz || makygo
|-
! non-exhaustive plural
| makmakɓi || makmakla || makmak || makmakbu || makmakydzh || makmakyz || makmakygo
|-
! exhaustive plural
| makylɓi || makylla || makyl || makylbu || makyldzh || makylz || makylgo
|}

=== Feminine ===

{| class="wikitable"
|+ ''mak'' — feminine declension
! Number !! Subject !! Object !! Intrans.
|-
! singular
| makɓi || makla || mak
|-
! non-exhaustive plural
| makmakɓi || makmakla || makmak
|-
! exhaustive plural
| makelɓi || makella || makel
|}

The possessive, instrumental and vocative cells of the feminine paradigm are not yet attested and are left open.

=== Masculine ===

{| class="wikitable"
|+ ''mak'' — masculine declension
! Number !! Subject !! Object !! Intrans.
|-
! singular
| makɓi || makla || mak
|-
! non-exhaustive plural
| makmakɓi || makmakla || makmak
|-
! exhaustive plural
| makalɓi || makalla || makal
|}

The exhaustive-plural connecting vowel tracks the gender: '''y''' for child, '''e''' for feminine, '''a''' for masculine.

== Vowel-ended paradigms ==

Not yet documented.

== Reduplicated stem ==

Not yet documented.

[[Category:Ringworld]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
